source: Road Home Program at Rush University Medical Center
note: Data compiled from publicly available information about the Road Home Program. Verify details at roadhomeprogram.org for the most current information.

pages.title: Road Home Program - Home
pages.content: The Road Home Program at Rush University Medical Center is a leading center for veterans and service members seeking treatment for invisible wounds of war including post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), traumatic brain injury (TBI), military sexual trauma (MST), anxiety, depression, and related conditions. The program provides world-class clinical care at no cost to veterans and their families regardless of discharge status or era of service. Founded in 2014 with support from the Wounded Warrior Project, the Road Home Program is part of the Rush University Medical Center system in Chicago, Illinois. The program offers intensive outpatient treatment, family services, and research to improve care for those who served.
key_info: Leading treatment center for veterans and service members with invisible wounds of war
Located at Rush University Medical Center in Chicago, Illinois
Treats PTSD, TBI, military sexual trauma, anxiety, depression, and related conditions
Services provided at no cost to veterans regardless of discharge status
Founded in 2014 with support from the Wounded Warrior Project
Offers intensive outpatient programs, family services, and ongoing research
No VA referral required
Serves veterans from all eras and all branches of service

pages.title: About Us - Road Home Program
pages.content: The Road Home Program at Rush University Medical Center provides world-class care for veterans and service members struggling with the invisible wounds of war. The program was established in 2014 at Rush University Medical Center with a foundational grant from the Wounded Warrior Project. It has since treated thousands of veterans from across the country. The program uses evidence-based treatments including Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT) and Prolonged Exposure (PE) therapy for PTSD. The Road Home Program is committed to removing barriers to care for veterans, including providing treatment at no cost, eliminating the need for VA referrals, and welcoming veterans regardless of discharge status, era of service, or branch. The program is part of Rush University Medical Center, a premier academic medical center in Chicago.
key_info: Established in 2014 at Rush University Medical Center
Founded with support from the Wounded Warrior Project
Has treated thousands of veterans from across the United States
Uses evidence-based treatments including CPT and Prolonged Exposure therapy
Committed to removing barriers to care for veterans
No cost to veterans and families for services
No VA referral needed
Welcomes all veterans regardless of discharge status or era of service
Part of Rush University Medical Center, a premier academic medical center in Chicago

pages.title: Family Center - Road Home Program
pages.content: The Road Home Program Family Center provides comprehensive support services for families of veterans and service members. The center recognizes that the invisible wounds of war affect the entire family, not just the service member. Family services include family therapy, couples counseling, parenting support, psychoeducation about PTSD and TBI, and support groups. The Family Center offers both in-person and telehealth options. Services are available to family members even if the veteran is not enrolled in the Road Home Program. The center helps families understand the impact of military service, develop communication skills, build resilience, and strengthen family relationships.
key_info: Comprehensive support services for families of veterans and service members
Recognizes that invisible wounds of war affect the entire family
Services include family therapy, couples counseling, and parenting support
Offers psychoeducation about PTSD and TBI for family members
Provides support groups for family members
Both in-person and telehealth options available
Family members can access services even if the veteran is not enrolled
Focuses on communication skills, resilience, and strengthening family relationships
All family services provided at no cost

pages.title: Contact Us - Road Home Program
pages.content: Contact the Road Home Program at Rush University Medical Center. Phone: (312) 942-8387. The Road Home Program is located at Rush University Medical Center in Chicago, Illinois. Veterans and family members can call to learn more about services, schedule an intake assessment, or ask questions about the program. The intake process begins with a phone call to the Road Home Program. Staff are available to answer questions and guide veterans through the enrollment process.
key_info: Located at Rush University Medical Center, Chicago, Illinois
Call to schedule an intake assessment
Staff available to answer questions about programs and services
Intake process begins with a phone call
Veterans and family members are welcome to call

pages.title: Treatment Programs - Road Home Program
pages.content: The Road Home Program offers several evidence-based treatment programs for veterans and service members. The Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) is a 3-week program that provides concentrated treatment for PTSD using evidence-based therapies including Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT) and Prolonged Exposure (PE) therapy. Veterans attend daily sessions and receive comprehensive care including individual therapy, group therapy, and complementary wellness activities. The program also addresses co-occurring conditions such as TBI, depression, anxiety, substance use, and military sexual trauma. Outpatient services are available for veterans who need ongoing care or who may benefit from a less intensive treatment schedule. The Road Home Program also offers specialized treatment tracks and programming tailored to the unique needs of veterans. All treatment is provided at no cost to the veteran.
key_info: Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP): 3-week concentrated treatment program for PTSD
Uses Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT) and Prolonged Exposure (PE) therapy
IOP includes daily individual therapy, group therapy, and wellness activities
Addresses co-occurring conditions: TBI, depression, anxiety, substance use, MST
Outpatient services available for ongoing or less intensive care
Specialized treatment tracks tailored to veteran needs
All treatment provided at no cost
Evidence-based approaches validated by research

pages.title: Get Care - Road Home Program
pages.content: Getting care at the Road Home Program is straightforward. The first step is to call (312) 942-8387 to speak with a member of the intake team. The intake team will conduct a phone screening to learn about the veteran's experiences and needs. Based on the screening, the team will recommend the most appropriate treatment track. Veterans do not need a referral from the VA or any other provider. The program serves veterans from all eras of service and all branches of the military, regardless of discharge status. There is no cost for treatment. Travel and lodging assistance may be available for veterans traveling from outside the Chicago area. The program welcomes veterans from across the country.
key_info: Step 1: Call (312) 942-8387 to speak with the intake team
Step 2: Phone screening to assess needs and experiences
Step 3: Team recommends appropriate treatment track
No VA referral required
No referral from any provider needed

All discharge statuses welcome including other-than-honorable

Travel and lodging assistance may be available for out-of-area veterans
Veterans from across the country are welcome

pages.title: Research - Road Home Program
pages.content: The Road Home Program is committed to advancing the science of treating invisible wounds of war through research. The program conducts clinical research to evaluate treatment outcomes and improve care for veterans with PTSD, TBI, and related conditions. Research areas include treatment efficacy, neuroimaging, biomarkers, and novel therapeutic approaches. The program collaborates with researchers at Rush University Medical Center and other institutions. Research findings are published in peer-reviewed journals and presented at national conferences. The program's research contributes to the broader understanding of PTSD and TBI treatment and helps inform evidence-based practices nationwide. Veterans may have the opportunity to participate in research studies during their treatment.

pages.title: Ways to Give - Road Home Program
pages.content: Support the Road Home Program through various giving options. Donations help fund treatment services for veterans at no cost. Ways to give include online donations, planned giving, corporate partnerships, and fundraising events. The Wounded Warrior Project is a major supporter of the program. All donations go toward providing treatment and support services for veterans and their families. The Road Home Program is part of Rush University Medical Center, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, making donations tax-deductible.
key_info: Online donations accepted
Planned giving options available
Corporate partnerships welcome
Fundraising events held to support the program
Wounded Warrior Project is a major supporter
Donations fund free treatment services for veterans
Part of Rush University Medical Center, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit
Donations are tax-deductible

pages.title: Our Team - Road Home Program
pages.content: The Road Home Program team consists of experienced clinicians, researchers, and support staff dedicated to serving veterans and their families. The clinical team includes psychiatrists, psychologists, social workers, and other mental health professionals with expertise in treating PTSD, TBI, and related conditions. Team members are trained in evidence-based therapies including Cognitive Processing Therapy and Prolonged Exposure therapy. The program leadership oversees clinical operations, research initiatives, and outreach efforts. Many team members have specialized training in military culture and the unique challenges faced by veterans.
key_info: Team includes psychiatrists, psychologists, social workers, and mental health professionals
Clinicians have expertise in PTSD, TBI, and related conditions
Staff trained in Cognitive Processing Therapy and Prolonged Exposure therapy
Program leadership oversees clinical operations and research
Many team members have specialized military culture training

Experienced in working with veteran populations

pages.title: Resources - Road Home Program
pages.content: The Road Home Program provides a collection of resources for veterans, service members, and their families. Resources include crisis hotlines, partner organizations, educational materials about PTSD and TBI, and links to additional support services. Key crisis resources: Veterans Crisis Line: 988 (press 1) or text 838255. National Suicide Prevention Lifeline: 988. Crisis Text Line: text HOME to 741741. Additional resources include the VA, Wounded Warrior Project, Give an Hour, Cohen Veterans Network, and other organizations serving veterans.
key_info: Veterans Crisis Line: 988 (press 1) or text 838255
National Suicide Prevention Lifeline: 988
Crisis Text Line: text HOME to 741741
Educational materials about PTSD and TBI available
Links to VA resources
Partner organizations: Wounded Warrior Project, Give an Hour, Cohen Veterans Network
Additional support services for veterans and families
Resources available for service members and family members

programs.name: Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP)
programs.description: A 3-week intensive outpatient program providing concentrated, evidence-based treatment for PTSD and related conditions. Includes daily individual therapy, group therapy, and complementary wellness activities. Uses Cognitive Processing Therapy (CPT) and Prolonged Exposure (PE) therapy.
programs.eligibility: Veterans and service members from all eras, all branches, and any discharge status. No VA referral required.

programs.name: Outpatient Services
programs.description: Ongoing outpatient care for veterans who need continued support or who may benefit from a less intensive treatment schedule. Provides individual therapy, medication management, and other support services.
programs.eligibility: Veterans and service members from all eras, all branches, and any discharge status. No VA referral required.

programs.name: Family Center Services
programs.description: Comprehensive support services for families of veterans and service members, including couples counseling, family therapy, parenting support, psychoeducation, and support groups. Available even if the veteran is not enrolled in the Road Home Program.
programs.eligibility: Family members of veterans and service members. Veteran does not need to be enrolled in the program.
programs.duration: Varies based on individual and family needs
